commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=74d8974a89adb3bb1db98b184ac5a6706f4d06bd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR defers implicit stale session rollover when the reply-run registry reports the same session key and session id as active, and adds focused tests for same-session and different-session active runs.

PR surface: Source +14, Tests +116. Total +130 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main archives stale daily sessions during `initSessionState` before later admission can wait behind an older active run. The PR also provides after-fix Crabbox proof for the intended same-session active-run case, but the introduced regression is source-proven from dispatch ordering.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR has a protected maintainer label plus a blocking correctness finding, so the next action is maintainer or contributor revision rather than an automatic repair lane.

target.title: fix: prevent daily rollover splitting active transcripts
target.body:
Closes #96546

AI-assisted: yes

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users with an active session run could have one running transcript split across files when an inbound message crossed the configured daily or idle reset boundary. The stale-session rollover archived the current transcript before reply-turn admission could steer, wait, or queue behind the active run, so the active writer recreated the old transcript path and left later output orphaned.

## Why This Change Was Made

Implicit daily/idle rollover now defers when the reply run registry shows the same session key and session id is actively running. That preserves the active transcript and lets normal admission behavior handle the inbound message; inactive stale sessions still archive and rotate as before, and explicit `/new`/`/reset` behavior is intentionally unchanged.

## User Impact

Users no longer lose or split active turn output when a message arrives just after a reset boundary while the previous turn is still running. Normal stale-session cleanup still works when no run owns the session.

## Evidence

Focused local proof:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts` - passed, 122 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/reply-turn-admission.test.ts` - passed, 10 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts` - passed, 60 tests.
- `git diff --check` - passed.

Live post-fix Crabbox proof:

- Azure Crabbox `cbx_23fe0be7ceb6`, run `run_ac9bd189fde6`, `Standard_D32ads_v6`, exit 0.
- Scenario seeded a stale daily-reset session with an active `replyRunRegistry` operation for the same session id, ran `initSessionState`, then appended the next transcript marker through the production JSONL append helper.
- Observed `isNewSession:false`, `resetTriggered:false`, `archiveFiles:[]`, store remained on `sess-old-active-96546`, and all transcript markers stayed in one file.

Changed gate:

- Azure Crabbox `cbx_be8fcb6177c8`, run `run_c97ba7f9822a`, `corepack pnpm check:changed` - passed, lanes `core, coreTests`.
target.author: joshavant
